name: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint
description: This plugin is used to pull indicators of type URL, IPv4, IPv6, Domain, MD5 and SHA256 from System > Settings > Endpoints > Indicators page of Microsoft Defender for Endpoint platform. This plugin supports sharing indicators of type URL, IPv4, IPv6, Domain, MD5 and SHA256 to System > Settings > Endpoints > Indicators page of Microsoft Defender for Endpoint platform. This plugin also supports pull and push retraction of indicators from Microsoft Defender for Endpoint platform.

configuration.label: Base URL
configuration.description: Base URL of the Microsoft Defender for Endpoint platform.

configuration.label: Tenant ID
configuration.description: Directory (Tenant) ID of the Microsoft Entra ID application. Navigate to Microsoft Entra Portal > App Registrations > [Your App] > Overview.

configuration.label: Application ID
configuration.description: Application (Client) ID of the Microsoft Entra ID application. Navigate to Microsoft Entra Portal > App Registrations > [Your App] > Overview.

configuration.label: Application Secret
configuration.description: Client Secret of the Microsoft Entra ID application. Navigate to Microsoft Entra Portal > App Registrations > [Your App] > Certificates & secrets.

configuration.label: IOC Source (Applicable only while sharing indicators)
configuration.description: The source where this indicator originated. This can be used for tracking where this indicator was defined. Limit 200 characters.

configuration.label: Type of Threat data to pull
configuration.description: Type of Threat data to pull. If no threat type is selected, all threat type indicators will be pulled.

configuration.label: Actions
configuration.description: Select the action(s) to filter the pulled indicators. If no action is selected, all indicators will be pulled regardless of their action.

configuration.label: Indicators with Generated Alert
configuration.description: Select whether to pull indicators based on their 'Generate Alert' flag. 'Both' pulls all indicators regardless of this flag.

configuration.label: Enable Polling
configuration.description: Enable/Disable polling data from Microsoft Defender for Endpoint. Disable if you only need to push indicators to Microsoft Defender for Endpoint.

configuration.label: Retraction Interval (in days)
configuration.description: Specify the number of days for which IoC retraction should be run for Microsoft Defender for Endpoint indicators. Note: This parameter is applicable only if "IoC(s) Retraction" is enabled in Threat Exchange Settings. Value must be between 1 and 365.

configuration.label: Initial Range (in days)
configuration.description: Number of days to pull historical indicators for the initial run. Value must be between 1 and 365.
